title: Best order to view the series and movies
display_title: Best order to view the series and movies
wikitext:
[]: Best order to view the series and movies             Quote Post by Number 44 » Mon May 24, 2010 10:20 pm I'm getting ready for a marathon viewing of Evangelion. Non stop, in a single day, with bathroom breaks taken while fly me to moon plays between episodes. Problem is in what order do I view? Before, I didn't have Death and Rebirth. I would view the series, directors cut up to episode 24, followed by EoE, and then back to 25, and 26. For this viewing, I had originally planned to do the series up to 24, followed by Death, skipping rebirth, and going straight to EoE. UNTIL , that is, I watched Death. It's continuity rift with the directors cut episodes makes my head hurt(Ep 23)! Also it incorporates dialog from EoTV. On the bright side, dub voices are the same between Death and Rebirth, and EoE. I welcome all suggestions on what order to view, what to leave out , what to include, and general thoughts on the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by Pman » Mon May 24, 2010 10:30 pm I would suggest watching them in the best sequential order you can possibly have. That way it makes the best sense. Could you please tell me what Eva DVDs/VHS you have? It would be simpler then. Make sure you have no interruptions: Close the blinds and curtains, tell you neighbors your out of town for the week, keep movement in wherever to a minimum, and also, move the DVD player next to you so you can keep siting in the same spot.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 24, 2010 10:31 pm It depends on if you think the endings are convergent or divergent. If you think the TV ending and The End of Evangelion are the same thing (part of the same whole): 1-20, 21'-24', The End of Evangelion If you think the TV ending and The End of Evangelion are NOT the same thing (similar but separate): 1-26; take a break and then watch 21'-24' and The End of Evangelion Death's primary merit is when you don't have the time to watch the entire series over again. You can probably skip it for some other time when you're planning to watch The End of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon May 24, 2010 10:37 pm Just set up two TVs next to each other and watch EoE and #25-#26 at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon May 24, 2010 11:04 pm what I did the first time around was watch the entire series (all 26 episodes), then proceeded to watch EoE IMO, Death is pointless, but the New Video Version episodes aren't (for now on, i'll just watch those)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue May 25, 2010 12:35 am Be sure to watch the extended/improved versions of episodes 21 through 24 (they're called "Director's Cut" on the US Platinum releases), they include a bunch of scenes that there wasn't enough time or money to put in there for the original airing. And don't skip episodes 25 and 26, no matter what you may have heard about them. Whether you want to consider them a complement of End of Evangelion, a similar but divergent story, or something entirely different, they're definitely worth watching. Er. Okay, I just reread your post and see that you've actually seen them before. D'oh. That renders my above explanation pointless, but I don't feel like deleting it. Either way, though, why skip them?

[]:             Quote Post by Hoshikawa-kun » Tue May 25, 2010 6:22 am I would actually suggest chronological order, rather than series order, starting with episode 21's flashback, then going to episodes 1-24, followed by EoE, stopping when Instrumentality begins to watch 25 and 26, then watching the rest of EoE. Just a crazy suggestion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 25, 2010 8:00 am EM nailed it, and I offer a vote for the second option.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue May 25, 2010 4:29 pm I'm definitely doing what Hoshikawa said once I have more free time.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Tue May 25, 2010 5:08 pm "Congratulations!"

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue May 25, 2010 8:44 pm I always watch episodes 1-24 and then EoE. Or Death then episodes 25-26. I've always thought the original ending suited being watched after Death because both of them have a kind of segmented feel. I tried merging these into one film with edits here and there... but I forgot about it

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Tue May 25, 2010 8:53 pm Definitely go for what TheEvaMonkey said. The TV versions of 21-24 are no longer necessary (though 25 and 26 TV is sort of necessary).

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Tue May 25, 2010 9:05 pm Episode 14 Episode 07 Episode 10 Episode 15 Episode 21 Episode 17 RoE 3.0: Q Episode 13 Episode 09 Episode 25 Episode 02 Episode 06 Episode 12 RoE Final Episode 23 Episode 05 Episode 26 Death and Rebirth Episode 18 Episode 20 RoE 1.0: Jo Episode 26' Episode 03 Episode 01 Episode 22 Episode 19 Episode 25' RoE 2.0: Ha Episode 08 Episode 11 Episode 04 Episode 16 Episode 24 There ya go.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue May 25, 2010 10:00 pm Legendary wrote: Episode 14 Episode 07 Episode 10 Episode 15 Episode 21 Episode 17 RoE 3.0: Q Episode 13 Episode 09 Eva Redeath Episode 25 Episode 02 Episode 06 Episode 12 RoE Final Episode 23 Episode 05 Episode 26 Death and Rebirth The Time Traveler's Wife Episode 18 Episode 20 RoE 1.0: Jo Episode 26' Episode 03 Episode 01 Episode 22 Episode 19 Episode 25' RoE 2.0: Ha Episode 08 Episode 11 Episode 04 Episode 16 Episode 24 Episode 07 Episode 07 Episode 07 Episode 07 Episode 07 Lost Series Finale There ya go.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue May 25, 2010 11:46 pm Ep. 1-20 21-24 Director's Cuts 25-26 EoE Finis.

[]:             Quote Post by AVman9 » Tue May 25, 2010 11:53 pm EPs 1-20 Director's 21-24 EoE 25-26 if you want to watch later on.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed May 26, 2010 10:00 pm Episode none of them through none of them, because Evangelion is terrible.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Wed May 26, 2010 10:03 pm Kyon order the first time, Haruhi the second.

[]:             Quote Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Wed May 26, 2010 10:39 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Ep. 1-20 21-24 Director's Cuts 25-26 EoE Finis.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu May 27, 2010 6:15 pm ZapX wrote: Kyon order the first time, Haruhi the second.
